name: Flutter Echo
explanation: A physically-modelled tape delay that foregrounds the wow and flutter of an unstable tape transport. The read head is modulated by a slow wow LFO plus a faster flutter LFO with a little drift, so every repeat is audibly pitch-warbled; each pass through the loop adds tape saturation and high-frequency loss, so echoes darken and degrade as they stack. The Warble control scales the modulation from subtle vintage warmth to seasick wobble. Controls: Time, Feedback, Warble, Tone and dry/wet Mix.
